response:
TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF EACH SUBSEQUENT CIRCLE INCREASES BY 10% FROM THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR². THE RADIUSES ARE r, 1.1r, 1.21r, 1.331r, 1.4641r, 1.61051r, 1.771561r, AND 1.9487171r. THE TOTAL AREA IS π(r² + (1.1r)² + (1.21r)² + (1.331r)² + (1.4641r)² + (1.61051r)² + (1.771561r)² + (1.9487171r)²). THIS EXPRESSION REPRESENTS THE TOTAL AREA IN TERMS OF r AND π.

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2π(1.4641r). THIS LENGTH IS USED TO SPACE TEXT CHARACTERS WITH A 0.5 CM GAP BETWEEN EACH. TO DETERMINE THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS, DIVIDE THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP SIZE. THE RESULT IS (2π(1.4641r)) / 0.5. THIS VALUE MUST BE AN INTEGER. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE FLOOR OF THIS CALCULATION. THE FINAL ANSWER IS THE INTEGER VALUE OBTAINED FROM THIS DIVISION. THE TEXT IS EVENLY DISTRIBUTED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE WITH NO OVERLAP AND A CONSISTENT GAP SIZE. THE DESIGN EMBRACES TRADITIONAL TYPOGRAPHY WHILE INTEGRATING MODERN MARKETING ELEMENTS INTO A VISUALLY STRIKING POSTER.
